languages.modeling_guidance: Prefer enum class, sealed hierarchies, data classes, and explicit domain types over raw command strings.
languages.localization_guidance: Separate CLI copy or UI strings from control flow so localization can evolve without rewriting logic.
languages.configuration_guidance: Use the platform's standard configuration and environment layering instead of ad-hoc constants.
documentation_topics: Kotlin language guide
Kotlin enum and sealed class patterns
Kotlin testing and Gradle or Maven integration
Mobile platform navigation and resource guidance
State, storage, and offline behavior guidance
Gradle project structure and task guidance
next_checks: Confirm new kotlin changes follow idiomatic modeling and naming conventions.
Review kotlin test layout and update the closest existing test surface first.
Check whether the platform has a first-class localization or resource mechanism before adding new user-facing copy.
Check whether the framework has a canonical way to model commands, routes, requests, or screens before inventing a custom pattern.

languages.modeling_guidance: Prefer dataclasses, typed models, modules, and explicit registries over implicit dict or string protocols.
languages.localization_guidance: Centralize UI or CLI copy in message helpers or translation catalogs rather than scattering literals.
languages.configuration_guidance: Use the platform's standard configuration and environment layering instead of ad-hoc constants.

Pytest and packaging practices

next_checks: Confirm new kotlin changes follow idiomatic modeling and naming conventions.
Review kotlin test layout and update the closest existing test surface first.
Check whether the platform has a first-class localization or resource mechanism before adding new user-facing copy.
Check whether the framework has a canonical way to model commands, routes, requests, or screens before inventing a custom pattern.
Confirm new python changes follow idiomatic modeling and naming conventions.
Review python test layout and update the closest existing test surface first.
Model commands, routes, and events with typed abstractions native to the language/platform.
Externalize user-facing copy, status text, and errors behind a localization/resource strategy.
Keep configuration, secrets, and environment handling aligned with the platform's standard settings model.
Verify tests and docs cover the latest feature flow before promoting the next brief version.
Only promote a new brief version after the final output is runnable, tested, and safe to review or roll back.
Preserve the last known-good active version when a newer brief version still has unresolved gaps.
Do not create the next brief version until the current project version is fully implemented and has no open gaps.
If a brief still has gaps, close them in the same brief version instead of pushing them into a newer brief.
When the project supports Docker review, make the latest promoted version runnable in Docker before asking for review.

current_focus.problem_statement: Multi-module Kotlin Android Compose app for Shopme buyer/seller flows, integrated with Shopme backend, media presigned upload, Hilt, Room, Firebase Messaging.
